entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 21:10
entries.excerpt: A certain prophet came down from Judea. He says from Judea because Caesarea, where they were staying, belongs to the region of Samaria, situated at the border of Phoenicia and Palestine.

entries.title: Homily on Acts 45 on acts 21:10
entries.excerpt: "And as we tarried there many days, there came down from Judea a certain prophet, named Agabus. And when he was come unto us, he took Paul's girdle, and bound his own hands and feet, and said, Thus saith the Holy Ghost, So shall the Jews at Jerusalem bind the man that owneth this girdle, and shall deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles." He who formerly had declared about the famine, the same says, This "man, who owneth this girdle, thus shall they bind." The same that the prophets used to do, representing events to the sight, when they spoke about the captivity - as did Ezekiel - the same did this Agabus. And Agabus does not say, "They shall bind" Paul, that he may not seem to speak upon agreement with Paul, but "the man that owneth this girdle" - so then he had a girdle also.

entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 21:10
entries.excerpt: Agabus, the one who long ago foretold the famine.

entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 21:10
entries.excerpt: This is that Agabus who earlier predicted the famine.
